C4H10 and C5H10
C2H4 and C4H10
C2H6 and C4H10
explanation:
In the given options, C2H6 (ethane) and C4H10 (butane) both belong to the same homologous series, as they have the same alkane functional group (CnH2n+2) and differ by a CH2 group:
C2H6: Ethane
C4H10: Butane
Both of these compounds are members of the alkane homologous series.

topics.title: ENVIRONMENTAL POLLUTION : AIR POLLUTION

subject: Chemistry
q: The pollutant usually presents in a city which generate its electricity from coal is
explanation: The pollutant usually present in a city that generates electricity from coal is sulphur (IV) oxide (SO2). When coal is burned, it releases various pollutants, including sulfur dioxide, which can contribute to air pollution and acid rain.

topics.title: ENVIRONMENTAL POLLUTION : AIR POLLUTION

subject: Chemistry
q: An organic compound which decolourizes bromine water is likely to be
explanation: An organic compound that decolorizes bromine water is likely to be an alkene, which is characterized by a carbon-carbon double bond. Among the options, C2H4 (ethylene) is an alkene and will react with bromine water, causing it to lose its color.

topics.title: ORGANIC COMPOUNDS : ALIPHATIC HYDROCARBONS

subject: Chemistry
q: Using the metal activity series, the metal that can liberate hydrogen gas from steam is
explanation: Using the metal activity series, iron can liberate hydrogen gas from steam because it is more reactive than hydrogen. When iron reacts with steam, it produces iron oxide and hydrogen gas.

topics.title: METALS AND THEIR COMPOUNDS : GENERAL CHARACTERISTICS AND PROPERTIES

subject: Chemistry
q: In which process is bauxite purified?
explanation: Bauxite is purified using the Bayer process, which involves the extraction of alumina (Al2O3) from bauxite ore using sodium hydroxide. This process is essential for producing aluminum.

topics.title: METALS AND THEIR COMPOUNDS : ORES

subject: Chemistry
q: Which acid is used to test for the presence of carbonates?
explanation: Hydrochloric acid (HCl) is used to test for the presence of carbonates. When a carbonate reacts with HCl, it produces carbon dioxide gas, which can be observed as effervescence (bubbles).

topics.title: ACID BASE AND SALTS : GENERAL CHARACTERISTICS AND PROPERTIES

subject: Chemistry
q: What is the main raw material for the Solvay process?
explanation: The main raw materials for the Solvay process are calcium carbonate (CaCO3) and sodium chloride (NaCl). The Solvay process is used to produce sodium carbonate (soda ash) from sodium chloride and limestone (which contains calcium carbonate).

topics.title: METALS AND THEIR COMPOUNDS : ALKALI METALS

subject: Chemistry
q: The process by which a liquid changes to gas below its boiling point is called
explanation: The process by which a liquid changes to gas below its boiling point is called evaporation. Evaporation occurs at any temperature and involves molecules at the surface of the liquid gaining enough energy to enter the gas phase.

topics.title: KINETIC THEORY OF MATTER AND GAS LAWS : AN OUTLINE OF THE KINETIC THEORY OF MATTER

subject: Chemistry
q: The process of converting vegetable oil to margarine is called
explanation: The process of converting vegetable oil to margarine is called hydrogenation. This process involves the addition of hydrogen to unsaturated fats, which converts them into saturated fats, resulting in a solid or semi-solid product like margarine.

topics.title: ORGANIC COMPOUNDS : GIANT MOLECULES

subject: Chemistry
q: What is the product of the complete combustion of hydrocarbons?
explanation: The product of the complete combustion of hydrocarbons is carbon dioxide (CO2) and water (H2O). During complete combustion, hydrocarbons react with oxygen to produce these two products, releasing energy in the process.

topics.title: CHEMICAL COMBINATION : COMBUSTION REACTIONS

subject: Chemistry
q: What is the expected product of the reaction between propene and cold dilute alkaline KMnO4
explanation: The expected product of the reaction between propene and cold dilute alkaline KMnO4 is propane-1,2-diol. This reaction is known as syn-dihydroxylation, where the alkene (propene) reacts with the oxidizing agent to form a diol (glycol).

topics.title: ORGANIC COMPOUNDS : ALIPHATIC HYDROCARBONS

subject: Chemistry
q: The polymer formed by the addition polymerization of tetrafluoroethene is used to make
explanation: The polymer formed by the addition polymerization of tetrafluoroethene is Teflon. Teflon, or polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE), is known for its non-stick properties and is widely used in cookware and other applications.

topics.title: ORGANIC COMPOUNDS : ALIPHATIC HYDROCARBONS

subject: Chemistry
q: What type of isomerism is exhibited by butanoic acid and methylpropanoic acid?
explanation: Butanoic acid and methylpropanoic acid exhibit functional group isomerism because they have the same molecular formula (C4H8O2) but different functional groups. Butanoic acid has a carboxylic acid functional group, while methylpropanoic acid has a branched structure with the same functional group.

topics.title: ORGANIC COMPOUNDS : ALKANOLS

subject: Chemistry
q: The immediate product of fermentation of glucose is
explanation: The immediate product of fermentation of glucose is ethanol. During fermentation, yeast converts glucose into ethanol and carbon dioxide under anaerobic conditions.

topics.title: ORGANIC COMPOUNDS : CARBOHYDRATES

subject: Chemistry
q: What is the name of the reaction in which benzene reacts with Cl2 in the presence of AlCl3?
explanation: The reaction in which benzene reacts with Cl2 in the presence of AlCl3 is called a substitution reaction. In this electrophilic aromatic substitution, one of the hydrogen atoms on the benzene ring is replaced by a chlorine atom.

topics.title: CHEMICAL COMBINATION : COMBUSTION REACTIONS

subject: Chemistry
q: The combustion of alkanes is generally accompanied by the formation of
explanation: The combustion of alkanes is generally accompanied by the formation of carbon dioxide (CO2) and water (H2O). This is a complete combustion reaction where alkanes react with oxygen to produce CO2 and H2O, releasing energy.

topics.title: ORGANIC COMPOUNDS : ALIPHATIC HYDROCARBONS

subject: Chemistry
q: When propan-2-ol is oxidized, the resulting compound is
explanation: When propan-2-ol (isopropanol) is oxidized, it typically forms propanone (acetone). This is because propan-2-ol is a secondary alcohol, and upon oxidation, secondary alcohols are converted to ketones. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Propanone'.

topics.title: ORGANIC COMPOUNDS : ALKANOLS

subject: Chemistry
q: An organic compound X contains only carbon and hydrogen. When 1 mole of X reacts completely with bromine, only 1 mole of bromine is consumed. This implies that X is
explanation: The fact that 1 mole of the organic compound X reacts with 1 mole of bromine indicates that X is likely an alkene. Alkenes have a double bond and can react with bromine in an addition reaction, consuming one mole of bromine. Alkanes would require more bromine due to their single bonds, while alkynes would consume more than one mole due to their triple bonds. Therefore, the correct answer is 'An alkene'.

topics.title: ORGANIC COMPOUNDS : ALIPHATIC HYDROCARBONS

subject: Chemistry
q: The major product formed when ethyne reacts with hydrogen in the presence of nickel is
explanation: When ethyne (acetylene) reacts with hydrogen in the presence of a catalyst like nickel, the major product formed is ethane. This reaction is a hydrogenation process where the triple bond in ethyne is converted to a single bond in ethane. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Ethane'.

topics.title: ORGANIC COMPOUNDS : ALIPHATIC HYDROCARBONS

subject: Chemistry
q: What is the role of concentrated H2SO4 in the esterification reaction?
explanation: In the esterification reaction, concentrated sulfuric acid (H2SO4) acts as a catalyst and a dehydrating agent. It helps to speed up the reaction between an alcohol and a carboxylic acid to form an ester while also removing water produced in the reaction, thus driving the equilibrium towards ester formation. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Catalyst and dehydrating agent'.

topics.title: ORGANIC COMPOUNDS : ALKANOLS

subject: Chemistry
q: Which reagent is used in the laboratory preparation of alkanes from alkanoates?
explanation: In the laboratory preparation of alkanes from alkanoates, sodium (Na) in the presence of ethanol is commonly used. This reaction involves the reduction of alkanoates to alkanes. Sodium acts as a reducing agent, and ethanol serves as a solvent. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Na and ethanol'.

topics.title: ORGANIC COMPOUNDS : ALIPHATIC HYDROCARBONS

subject: Chemistry
q: Which of these is not a characteristic of aromatic compounds?
explanation: Aromatic compounds are characterized by having a cyclic structure with alternating double bonds, resonance structures, and delocalized π-electrons. However, they do not have saturated carbon atoms, as saturated carbon atoms would imply single bonds only. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Saturated carbon atoms'.

topics.title: ORGANIC COMPOUNDS : AROMATIC HYDROCARBONS

subject: Chemistry
q: Which reaction will give an alcohol as the only organic product?
explanation: The hydrolysis of an ester involves the reaction of the ester with water, resulting in the formation of an alcohol and a carboxylic acid. This reaction can yield an alcohol as the only organic product if the ester is hydrolyzed completely. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Hydrolysis of an ester'.

topics.title: ORGANIC COMPOUNDS : ALKANOLS

subject: Chemistry
q: Which of the following best describes the reaction of propene with HBr?
explanation: The reaction of propene with HBr is best described as an electrophilic addition reaction. In this reaction, the HBr molecule adds across the double bond of propene, resulting in the formation of a bromoalkane. Electrophilic addition involves the attack of an electrophile (H+) on the nucleophilic double bond. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Electrophilic addition'.

topics.title: ORGANIC COMPOUNDS : ALIPHATIC HYDROCARBONS

subject: Chemistry
q: Which pair of compounds can be used to prepare ethyl ethanoate?
explanation: Ethyl ethanoate (also known as ethyl acetate) can be prepared by the reaction of ethanol and acetic acid through a process called esterification. This reaction involves the condensation of the alcohol and the carboxylic acid, resulting in the formation of the ester and water. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Ethanol and acetic acid'.

topics.title: ORGANIC COMPOUNDS : ALKANOATES

subject: Chemistry
q: Which of these cannot be a product of petroleum cracking?
explanation: Petroleum cracking is a process used to break down larger hydrocarbon molecules into smaller ones, typically producing alkanes and alkenes. Products of petroleum cracking include propane, ethene, and methane. However, glucose is a carbohydrate and not a product of petroleum cracking, which focuses on hydrocarbons. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Glucose'.

topics.title: HYDROCARBON

subject: Chemistry
q: Which of the following is not a separation technique?
explanation: Hydration is not a separation technique; it refers to the process of water molecules surrounding solute particles. The other options (Distillation, Evaporation, and Filtration) are all methods used to separate mixtures.

topics.title: SEPARATION OF MIXTURES AND PURIFICATION OF CHEMICAL : SEPARATION PROCESS

subject: Chemistry
q: The crystal layer of graphite are held together by
explanation: The crystal layers of graphite are held together by electrostatic forces, specifically van der Waals forces, which are weak attractions between the layers. Covalent bonds exist within the layers, but not between them.

topics.title: ATOMIC STRUCTURE AND BONDING : CHEMICAL BONDING

subject: Chemistry
q: Which off the following elements has the highests first ionization energy?
explanation: Among the given elements, Calcium has the highest first ionization energy. Ionization energy generally increases across a period and decreases down a group in the periodic table. Calcium is in Group 2 and has a higher ionization energy than the other alkali metals listed.

topics.title: ATOMIC STRUCTURE AND BONDING : THE PERIODIC TABLE AND PERIODICITY OF ELEMENTS

subject: Chemistry
q: If 200cm3 of a certain gas weighs 0.5g at s.t.p., calculate its molar mass. [1.0 mole of a gas at s.t.p. occupies 22.4dm3]
explanation: To calculate the molar mass, we use the formula: Molar Mass = (mass of gas in grams / volume of gas in dm^3) * 22.4 dm^3. Here, Molar Mass = (0.5 g / 0.2 dm^3) * 22.4 dm^3 = 56 g/mol.

topics.title: CHEMICAL COMBINATION : THE MOLE CONCEPT AND AVOGADRO'S NUMBER

subject: Chemistry
q: Calculate the oxidation number of sulphur in Na2S2O6
explanation: In Na2S2O6, the oxidation number of sodium (Na) is +1, and oxygen (O) is -2. Let the oxidation number of sulfur (S) be x. The equation is: 2(+1) + 2x + 6(-2) = 0, which simplifies to 2 + 2x - 12 = 0, leading to 2x = 10, thus x = +5.

topics.title: OXIDATION AND REDUCTION : OXIDATION

subject: Chemistry
q: WHen SO2 gas is passed into a solution of iron (III) tetraoxosulphate (VI), the reaction mixture turns
explanation: When SO₂ gas is passed into a solution of iron (III) tetraoxosulphate (VI), it reduces the iron (III) ions to iron (II) ions, resulting in a color change. The solution turns brown due to the formation of iron (II) ions, which have a brownish color.

topics.title: CHEMICAL COMBINATION : ELEMENTS COMPOUNDS AND MIXTURES

subject: Chemistry
q: In the production of sugar, impurities can be removed by
explanation: In the production of sugar, crystallization is a common method used to purify sugar by allowing the sugar to form crystals while leaving impurities in the solution. This process effectively separates the sugar from unwanted substances.

topics.title: SEPARATION OF MIXTURES AND PURIFICATION OF CHEMICAL : SEPARATION PROCESS

subject: Chemistry
q: A metal used as an electrode during electrolysis is
explanation: Platinum is commonly used as an electrode during electrolysis due to its excellent conductivity and resistance to corrosion. It does not react with the electrolytic solution, making it ideal for this purpose.

topics.title: METALS AND THEIR COMPOUNDS : GENERAL CHARACTERISTICS AND PROPERTIES

subject: Chemistry
q: 400cm3 of gas X diffused through a porous pot in 2 minutes. Calculate the rate in cm3s-1 at which X diffuses
explanation: To calculate the rate of diffusion, we divide the volume of gas by the time taken. Here, 400 cm³ of gas X diffused in 2 minutes (120 seconds), so the rate is 400 cm³ / 120 s = 3.33 cm³/s.

topics.title: GAS LAW

subject: Chemistry
q: In packaged foods, nitrogen is used as a preservative to prevent
explanation: Nitrogen is used in packaged foods as a preservative to prevent rancidity. It displaces oxygen in the packaging, which helps to slow down the oxidation process that can lead to spoilage and rancid flavors.

topics.title: ENVIRONMENTAL POLLUTION : SOURCES AND EFFECTS OF POLLUTANTS

subject: Chemistry
q: An example of a substance which increases in mass when heated in air is
explanation: When magnesium ribbon is heated in air, it reacts with oxygen to form magnesium oxide, which has a greater mass than the original magnesium. This is due to the addition of oxygen from the air during the reaction.

topics.title: METALS AND THEIR COMPOUNDS : GENERAL CHARACTERISTICS AND PROPERTIES

subject: Chemistry
q: Which of the following products is liberated at the top of a fractionating column during the fractional distillation of crude oil?
explanation: Heptane is one of the lighter fractions that is typically collected at the top of a fractionating column during the fractional distillation of crude oil. The lighter hydrocarbons have lower boiling points and thus vaporize first.

topics.title: HYDROCARBON

subject: Chemistry
q: Aluminium is preferred to copper in overhead power cables because it
explanation: Aluminium is preferred to copper in overhead power cables because it is a good conductor of electricity and is much lighter than copper, making it easier to handle and install. Additionally, aluminium is more cost-effective.

topics.title: METALS AND THEIR COMPOUNDS : ALUMINIUM

subject: Chemistry
q: Alkali metals exhibit similar chemical properties because
options: they have the same number of valence electrons
explanation: Alkali metals exhibit similar chemical properties because they have the same number of valence electrons (one electron in their outermost shell). This similarity in electron configuration leads to similar reactivity and bonding characteristics.

topics.title: METALS AND THEIR COMPOUNDS : ALKALI METALS

subject: Chemistry
q:
The element with electronic configururation
1s22s22p63s23p64s2 is
explanation: The electronic configuration 1s22s22p63s23p64s2 corresponds to Calcium (Ca), which has an atomic number of 20. This configuration indicates that it has 20 electrons, matching the atomic structure of calcium.

topics.title: ATOMIC STRUCTURE AND BONDING : ATOMIC STRUCTURE ELECTRON CONFIGURATION ATOMIC NUMBER MASS NUMBER AND ISOTOPES

subject: Chemistry
q: When acid is dissolved in water to give hydroxonium ions, water functions as
explanation: When acid is dissolved in water, it donates protons (H⁺ ions) to water molecules, forming hydroxonium ions (H₃O⁺). In this process, water acts as a proton donor, which is a characteristic behavior of Brønsted-Lowry acids and bases.

topics.title: ACID BASE AND SALTS : GENERAL CHARACTERISTICS AND PROPERTIES

subject: Chemistry
q: When ∆H of a reaction is negative, the reaction is said to be
explanation: A reaction with a negative ∆H indicates that heat is released during the reaction, which classifies it as exothermic. In exothermic reactions, the products have lower energy than the reactants, resulting in the release of energy, typically in the form of heat.

topics.title: ENERGY CHANGES : ENDOTHERMIC AND EXOTHERMIC REACTIONS

subject: Chemistry
q:
Calculate the pressure of a 0.5 mole oxygen gas occupying a volume of 2.50 dm3 at 27oC.
[R = 0.0820 atmdm3 mol-1K-1]
explanation: To calculate the pressure of the gas, we can use the Ideal Gas Law: PV = nRT. Rearranging gives P = nRT/V. Here, n = 0.5 moles, R = 0.0820 atm dm³ mol⁻¹ K⁻¹, T = 27°C = 300 K, and V = 2.50 dm³. Plugging in the values: P = (0.5 moles * 0.0820 atm dm³ mol⁻¹ K⁻¹ * 300 K) / 2.50 dm³ = 4.92 atm.

topics.title: GAS LAW

subject: Chemistry
q: Which of the following supports combustion?
explanation: Chlorine supports combustion as it is a strong oxidizing agent. It can react with combustible materials, facilitating the combustion process. Other options like Boron, Hydrogen, and Sodium do not primarily support combustion in the same way.

topics.title: CHEMICAL COMBINATION : COMBUSTION REACTIONS

subject: Chemistry
q: The element that reacts explosively with water is
explanation: Sodium reacts explosively with water, producing sodium hydroxide and hydrogen gas, which can ignite due to the heat of the reaction. This is a well-known characteristic of alkali metals, particularly sodium.

topics.title: METALS AND THEIR COMPOUNDS : ALKALI METALS
